title: build, heredoc: show heredoc summary in build output
body:
Fixes cases missed by https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/5261

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *BoleynSu* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign [tomsweeneyredhat](https://github.com/tomsweeneyredhat) for approval. For more information see [the Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Which cases are being missed?  As near as I can tell, this switches to always calculating the bits to append to the log message to include a mention of heredoc contents, even in cases when the log message isn't going to be output, when we didn't bother for those cases before.

My bad. I thought this `if not quiet` also guard the heredoc processing fix in the PR  I linked. My fix can be ignored. The reason I try to "fix" it is the same reason as linked PR though. Somehow `buildah build` is using wrong cache for run heredoc.

Looks like when I run git blame on the `main` branch I was led to a different PR as you can tell from the PR title. The issue I saw was already fixed by https://github.com/containers/buildah/commit/4dd300b1a7d666ec7bf7772a449e637c06032332
